chunks.text: Ming C. Wu is Nortel Distinguished Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at University of California, Berkeley, and Co-Director of Berkeley Sensor and Actuator Center (BSAC) and Berkeley Emerging Technologies Research Center (BETR). He received B.S. from National Taiwan University in 1983 and Ph.D. from UC Berkeley in 1988. Prior to joining the faculty at Berkeley, Dr. Wu has conducted research at AT&T Bell Laboratories in Murray Hill, New Jersey (1988-1992) and the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) (1992-2004).
chunks.question: What are Ming C. Wu's academic positions and research centers?
chunks.answer: Professor and co-director of BSAC and BETR.

chunks.text: Dr. Wu has published over 600 papers in journals and conferences and holds 35 US patents in Optical MEMS, optofluidics, silicon photonics and optoelectronics. In 1997, Prof. Wu co-founded OMM, Inc. to commercialize MEMS optical switches. OMM became the first company to supply Telcordia-qualified 8x8 and 16x16 switches. Recently, Prof. Wu has pushed the size of optical switches to 240x240 by combining MEMS with silicon photonics.
chunks.question: What are Ming C. Wu's key research contributions and patents?
chunks.answer: Over 600 papers and 35 patents in optical technologies.

chunks.text: In 2011, Prof. Wu co-founded Berkeley Lights, Inc. (NASDAQ:BLI) to commercialize optoelectronic tweezers (OET) invented by his group. The OET instrument is capable of sorting, cloning, culturing, analyzing antibody secretions of tens of thousands of single cells on a single optofluidic chip. The OET instruments have been used by major pharmaceutical companies for antibody discovery, cell line development, and synthetic biology.
chunks.question: What company did Ming C. Wu co-found in 2011 and for what purpose?
chunks.answer: Berkeley Lights to commercialize optoelectronic tweezers.

chunks.text: Prof. Wu's accomplishments have been recognized by the Paul F. Forman Engineering Excellence Award (OSA 2007), the William Streifer Scientific Achievement Award (IEEE Photonics Society 2016), the C.E.K. Mees Medal (OSA 2017), the Robert Bosch Micro and Nano Electro Mechanical Systems Award (IEEE Electronic Device Society, 2020), and the Bakar Prize (UC Berkeley 2021). Prof. Wu is Fellow of IEEE and Optical Society (OSA), a Packard Foundation Fellow (1992 – 1997), and a Bakar Fellow (2018-2019).
chunks.question: What major awards has Ming C. Wu received for his work?
chunks.answer: Multiple awards including Bakar Prize and IEEE Fellow.

chunks.text: Education: 1988, PhD, EECS, University of California, Berkeley. Research Areas: Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems (MEMS), Physical Electronics (PHY), Si photonics, optoelectronics, nanophotonics, optical MEMS, Optofluidics. Research Centers: Berkeley Emerging Technologies Research (BETR), Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center (BSAC), NSF Challenge Institute for Quantum Computation (CIQC).
chunks.question: What are Ming C. Wu's education and primary research areas?
chunks.answer: PhD from UC Berkeley in MEMS and photonics.
